**You MUST use CLI commands for lesson management:**

| Command | Purpose |

You MUST call `npx ca search` BEFORE:

- User corrects you ("no", "wrong", "actually...")

- Test fails then you fix it

**Quality gate** (must pass before capturing):
- Novel (not already stored)
- Specific (clear guidance)
- Actionable (preferred, not mandatory)

**Workflow**: Search BEFORE deciding, capture AFTER learning.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-3tn [EPIC] · Polish: Resilience and data safety   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-29 · Updated: 2026-03-29

DESCRIPTION
The app has no error boundary and uses in-memory session storage. A single component error crashes the entire UI with no recovery path, and all session data is lost on refresh.

**Findings to fix**:
1. **ErrorBoundary (P0)**: No React ErrorBoundary exists anywhere. Add at App level wrapping all providers, and at DawShell level wrapping the complex panel tree. Include graceful fallback UI with reload/recovery options.
   - Files: src/App.tsx, src/ui/DawShell.tsx
   
2. **Session persistence (P1)**: DawShell.tsx:23-24 uses createInMemorySessionStorage() with a comment saying 'temporary'. Wire IndexedDB session storage from App-level provider. The IndexedDB implementation already exists in session-storage.ts.
   - Files: src/ui/DawShell.tsx, src/state/session/session-storage.ts, src/App.tsx

3. **AudioContext error handling (P2)**: engine-context.ts has no try-catch around ctx.resume() or ctx.audioWorklet.addModule(). Failures propagate uncaught. Add error handling with user-facing recovery hints.
   - Files: src/audio/engine-context.ts

**Acceptance criteria**:
- ErrorBoundary catches render errors and shows recovery UI (not white screen)
- Session data survives page refresh via IndexedDB
- AudioContext failures show actionable error messages
- All existing tests still pass

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-ba9: Polish: QA verification via browser automation ● P1

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the thorough exploration report.

---

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT: Resilience and Data Safety Architecture

I have thoroughly explored the long-running-harness codebase. Here's the complete analysis:

### 1. ErrorBoundary Situation

**Status: NO ErrorBoundary exists**

- No React ErrorBoundary components found anywhere in the codebase (`grep -r "ErrorBoundary"` returns nothing)
- The application uses a different error handling pattern: **conditional rendering based on state**
- Current error states are handled in `App.tsx` with:
  - `CrossOriginError` component (for missing SharedArrayBuffer)
  - `ClickToStart` component (for engine initialization)
  - Full conditional rendering chains instead of boundary catch semantics

**Component tree structure from src/App.tsx:**
```
App
├── CrossOriginError (if !crossOriginOk)
├── ClickToStart (if engine === null || pool === null)
└── AudioEngineProvider → MediaPoolProvider → DawShell
    └── TransportProvider → EffectsBridgeProvider → RoutingBridgeProvider → DawShellInner
        ├── Toolbar
        ├── ArrangementPanel
        ├── MixerPanel
        ├── InstrumentPanel
        ├── MediaPoolPanel
        └── RecoveryDialog (for session recovery warnings)
```

**Full contents of src/App.tsx:**
Already provided above (lines 1-92)

**Full contents of src/ui/DawShell.tsx:**
Already provided above (lines 1-97)

**Key finding:** The error handling is deterministic and state-based. There are no try-catch boundaries. Errors are:
- Caught at the source (e.g., `App.tsx` lines 46-51 when creating audio engine)
- Propagated through promise rejection handlers
- Converted to state changes (engineStatus)
- Rendered conditionally

---

### 2. Session Storage Implementation

#### In-Memory Storage (Test/Current)

**File: src/state/session/session-storage.ts (lines 22-83)**

Uses two Maps:
- `active`: tracks draft, current, and backup
- `saved`: stores full session history with metadata

This is the current implementation used by DawShell (line 24: `createInMemorySessionStorage()`).

#### IndexedDB Storage (Production-Ready)

**File: src/state/session/session-storage.ts (lines 180-270)**

Provides persistent storage with:
- **STORE_ACTIVE**: draft, current, backup (keys: "draft", "current", "backup")
- **STORE_SAVED**: named sessions (keys: session IDs)
- **Database**: "brutalwav-sessions", version 1
- **Error handling**: Promise-based with rejection paths for all IDB operations

**Critical architecture detail from line 23-24 in DawShell.tsx:**
```typescript
// Use in-memory storage until IndexedDB is wired in App-level provider
const defaultStorage = createInMemorySessionStorage();
```

This indicates the IndexedDB implementation exists but is NOT YET integrated at the App level.

#### Media Pool Storage

**File: src/audio/media-pool/idb-storage.ts (lines 1-242)**

Separate IndexedDB instance for audio assets:
- **DB_NAME**: "brutalwav-media"
- **Stores**: STORE_BLOBS, STORE_META, STORE_PEAKS
- Handles audio source metadata, waveform peaks, and blob storage

---

### 3. AudioContext Error Handling

**File: src/audio/engine-context.ts (FULL CONTENTS)**

```typescript
export type AudioEngineContext = {
  readonly ctx: AudioContext;
  readonly state: AudioEngineState;
  resume: () => Promise<void>;
  close: () => Promise<void>;
  loadWorkletModule: (url: string) => Promise<void>;
};

export function createAudioEngine(): AudioEngineContext {
  const ctx = new AudioContext();

  const engine: AudioEngineContext = {
    ctx,
    get state(): AudioEngineState {
      switch (ctx.state) {
        case "suspended": return "suspended";
        case "running": return "running";
        case "closed": return "closed";
        default: return "uninitialized";
      }
    },
    async resume(): Promise<void> {
      if (ctx.state === "suspended") {
        await ctx.resume();
      }
    },
    async close(): Promise<void> {
      if (ctx.state !== "closed") {
        await ctx.close();
      }
    },
    async loadWorkletModule(url: string): Promise<void> {
      await ctx.audioWorklet.addModule(url);
    },
  };

  return engine;
}
```

**Current error handling status:**
- `createAudioEngine()` constructor throws are caught in App.tsx (lines 46-51)
- `ctx.resume()` rejection is caught in App.tsx (lines 54-67)
- `ctx.audioWorklet.addModule()` has **NO error handling** - will throw synchronously if module fails
- **No retry logic exists for any of these operations**
- No graceful degradation paths

**Test coverage from src/audio/engine-context.test.ts:**
Tests exist for happy paths and basic state transitions, but not for:
- Module loading failures
- Resume rejection scenarios
- Worklet module network errors

---

### 4. Test Setup and Infrastructure

**Framework: Vitest** (not Jest)

**Configuration: vitest.config.ts (lines 1-27)**
- Environment: jsdom
- Globals enabled
- Setup file: src/test/setup.ts
- Coverage provider: v8

**Setup file: src/test/setup.ts**
```typescript
import "@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest";
```
Minimal setup - just DOM matchers.

**Test utilities:**
- `@testing-library/react` (v16.3.2)
- `@testing-library/user-event` (v14.6.1)
- `@testing-library/jest-dom` (v6.9.1)
- `vitest` (v4.1.1)

**Test patterns found:**

1. **Component tests** (src/App.test.tsx, src/ui/DawShell.test.tsx):
   - Mock AudioContext with custom class
   - Mock providers with identity pass-through
   - Use render/fireEvent/act pattern
   - Test state mutations via store getState()

2. **Hook tests** (src/state/session/use-session-persistence.test.ts):
   - renderHook from @testing-library/react

   - Advance timers with vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync
   - Test async loading and auto-save behavior

3. **Function tests** (src/state/session/session-recovery.test.ts):
   - Pure function testing
   - JSON round-tripping
   - Partial recovery validation

**Mocking pattern used:**

  const actual = await importOriginal<typeof import("@audio/media-pool/idb-storage")>();
  return {

```

### 6. Session Persistence & Recovery Architecture

**Three-tier safety system currently implemented:**

#### Tier 1: Save Queue (src/state/session/save-queue.ts)
- **Crash-safe write pattern:**
  1. Get previous current
  2. Write to draft
  3. Write to current
  4. Write previous to backup
  5. Delete draft
- **Inflight tracking:** One save at a time, queues next while in-flight
- **Error handling:** Queued saves are cleared on error (data loss risk!)

#### Tier 2: Auto-Save (src/state/session/auto-save.ts)
- **Debounce:** 2000ms (configurable)
- **Interval:** 30,000ms = 30 seconds (configurable)
- **Dirty tracking:** Boolean flag
- **No error recovery:** If saveQueue.enqueue() throws, exception is unhandled

#### Tier 3: Session Recovery (src/state/session/session-recovery.ts)
- **Partial recovery:** Parse each section independently with Zod
- **Warnings collected:** List of failed sections returned to UI
- **Display:** RecoveryDialog shows warnings, offers "Continue with recovered session" or "Start fresh"

#### Integration in App

export type AudioEngineContext = {
  readonly ctx: AudioContext;
  readonly state: AudioEngineState;
  resume: () => Promise<void>;
  close: () => Promise<void>;
  loadWorkletModule: (url: string) => Promise<void>;
};

export function createAudioEngine(): AudioEngineContext {
  const ctx = new AudioContext();

  const engine: AudioEngineContext = {
    ctx,
    get state(): AudioEngineState {
      switch (ctx.state) {
        case "suspended": return "suspended";
        case "running": return "running";
        case "closed": return "closed";
        default: return "uninitialized";
      }
    },
    async resume(): Promise<void> {
      if (ctx.state === "suspended") {
        await ctx.resume();
      }
    },
    async close(): Promise<void> {
      if (ctx.state !== "closed") {
        await ctx.close();
      }
    },
    async loadWorkletModule(url: string): Promise<void> {
      await ctx.audioWorklet.addModule(url);
    },
  };

  return engine;
}
```
